Channel.title: Channel
Channel.description: Represents a TV channel on Pocketnews TV.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Channel entity.

createdAt.description: The date and time the channel was created.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video uploaded to Pocketnews TV.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

uploadDate.description: The date and time the video was uploaded.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of Pocketnews TV.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user is an administrator.

isCreator.description: Indicates if the user is a content creator.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a content category for videos.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a user-submitted report about a video.

videoId.description: The ID of the reported video.

videoTitle.description: The title of the reported video (denormalized).

userId.description: The ID of the user who submitted the report.

details.description: Additional details provided by the user.

createdAt.description: The date and time the report was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user data, including email, display name, and admin status.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores channel data including name, description, and creation date.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the channel.

definition.description: Stores video data, including title, description, thumbnailUrl, and upload date. This is a top-level collection for efficient querying.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier of the video.

definition.description: Indicates admin role by document existence. Document content is irrelevant. (DBAC).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the report.

definition.description: Stores email messages to be sent by a backend service.

params.name: emailId
params.description: The unique identifier of the email message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support Pocketnews TV's core features, focusing on channel and video management, user roles, and admin analytics. To ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, the design adheres to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). 

**Authorization Independence:** Videos are now in a top-level collection to simplify homepage and category-based queries, which is a common and efficient pattern. The `channelId` field is maintained on each video document to link back to the channel, allowing for easy data joins on the client and for authorization rules that might depend on channel ownership in the future.

**Structural Segregation:**  The structure segregates `videos`, `channels`, `users`, `categories`, `reports`, and `email_queue` into their own top-level collections. This simplifies security rules and makes the data model easier to understand. Each collection can have its own distinct security posture.

**Access Modeling:** Private user data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, using path-based ownership. Admin roles are managed via the existence of documents in `/roles_admin/{userId}`, representing global roles using DBAC principles. 

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Placing videos in a top-level collection is essential for performant listing on the main pages. Firestore queries are shallow and can only query a single collection at a time (unless a collection group query is used, which adds complexity). By having a single `videos` collection, the homepage can easily query for the latest videos or filter by category across all channels. Security rules for the `videos` collection simply allow `list` and `get` for all users, which is appropriate for public video content.
